task_completion.description: Verify the four required layers are created with the correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel in LibreCAD. You should see layers named 'POWER' (color set to Red), 'RUNGS' (color set to Yellow), 'SYMBOLS' (color set to Green), and 'TEXT' (color set to White/Black).

task_completion.description: Verify the vertical power rails are drawn on the POWER layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two long vertical red lines in the main drawing canvas, representing the L1 and L2 power rails.

task_completion.description: Verify the three horizontal rungs are drawn on the RUNGS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three horizontal yellow lines connecting the two vertical red rails, representing the three rungs of the ladder diagram.

task_completion.description: Verify the component symbols are drawn on the SYMBOLS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for green shapes (circles, arcs, or line combinations) placed along the horizontal rungs. Rung 1 should have symbols for Stop, Start, and Motor Coil. Rung 2 should have Aux Contact and Pilot Light. Rung 3 should have Overload Contact and Alarm Indicator.

task_completion.description: Verify the text labels and title are added on the TEXT layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for white/black text on the canvas. You should see labels like 'L1', 'L2', 'STOP', 'START', 'M1', 'PL1', 'OL', 'AL1', and a title containing 'MOTOR CONTROL - CONVEYOR M1' (or at least the word 'MOTOR').

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog. It should show the filename 'motor_control_ladder.dxf' indicating the file has been saved to the expected path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the LibreCAD application open with visible toolbars, layer panels, and a drawing canvas, rather than just a terminal window running a script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively drew the components rather than loading a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for signs of active drawing in the LibreCAD command line history (at the bottom of the screen), showing commands like 'line', 'circle', 'layer', or 'text' being executed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually write DXF code in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with raw DXF code being pasted or typed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/motor_control_ladder.dxf. Required layers and their standard colors: POWER=Red, RUNGS=Yellow, SYMBOLS=Green, TEXT=White/Black. The drawing should contain at least 20 entities. Note: While the task specifies the title 'MOTOR CONTROL - CONVEYOR M1', the underlying verifier only strictly checks for the presence of the word 'MOTOR' in the text entities, so accept if 'MOTOR' is present.
